---
title: "Elastic Model"
output:
  pdf_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
    number_sections: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: '2'
    df_print: paged
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(dplyr)
library(factoextra)
library(here)
library(sf)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
list.files(here(), recursive = TRUE)

tract_data    <- readRDS(here("output", "tract_data.rds"))
base_incident_data <- read.csv(here("output", "clustered_incident_data.csv"))


##ibm colorblind palette
ibm_blue    <- "#648FFF"
ibm_purple  <- "#785EF0"
ibm_magenta <- "#DC267F"
ibm_orange  <- "#FE6100"
ibm_gold    <- "#FFB000"

##cluster labels and palette
cluster_labels <- c(
  "1" = "Low Disadvantage",
  "2" = "Moderate Disadvantage",
  "3" = "High Disadvantage",
  "4" = "Highest Disadvantage"
)

ibm_palette <- c(
  "Low Disadvantage"      = ibm_purple,
  "Moderate Disadvantage" = ibm_blue,
  "High Disadvantage"     = ibm_orange,
  "Highest Disadvantage"  = ibm_magenta
)

##styling
theme_ibm <- function() {
  theme_light() +
  theme(
    plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle    = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "#E8EEFF", color = NA),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10, color = "#1a1a1a"),
    axis.text        = element_text(size = 8),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90")
  )
}
```

```{r elastic-net, cache=TRUE}
# Elastic Net Models (RQ1)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(cache = FALSE)

library(dplyr)
library(glmnet)
library(caret)
library(pROC)
library(MLmetrics)
library(mltools)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)
library(doParallel)

# Load Data
clustered_incident_data = read.csv(here("output", "clustered_incident_data.csv"))

# Predictors
predictors = c(
  "median_income",
  "gini",
  "poverty_rate",
  "unemployment_rate",
  "no_vehicle_rate",
  "housing_burden_rate",
  "vacancy_rate",
  "renter_rate",
  "single_parent_rate",
  "lang_isolation_rate",
  "internet_rate",
  "edu_rate_no_hsdip",
  "edu_rate_college",
  "disability_rate",
  "cluster"
)


# Part 1: Binary (Violent vs Property)


enet_data <- clustered_incident_data |>
  select(is_violent, all_of(predictors)) |>
  na.omit()

enet_data$is_violent <- factor(
  ifelse(enet_data$is_violent, 1, 0),
  levels = c(0, 1)
)

x <- model.matrix(is_violent ~ ., data = enet_data)[, -1]
y <- enet_data$is_violent

set.seed(123)
train_idx <- createDataPartition(y, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)

x_train <- x[train_idx, ]
x_test  <- x[-train_idx, ]
y_train <- y[train_idx]
y_test  <- y[-train_idx]

cv_enet <- cv.glmnet(
  x_train, y_train,
  family       = "binomial",
  alpha        = 0.5,
  nfolds       = 5,
  type.measure = "auc"
)
plot(
  cv_enet,
  main = expression(
    "Cross-Validated Elastic Net (" * alpha * " = 0.5)"
  )
)
best_lambda <- cv_enet$lambda.1se
cat("Optimal Lambda:", best_lambda, "\n")

elastic_net <- glmnet(
  x_train, y_train,
  family = "binomial",
  alpha  = 0.5,
  lambda = best_lambda
)

pred_prob <- as.vector(
  predict(
    elastic_net,
    newx = x_test,
    s    = best_lambda,
    type = "response"
  )
)

roc_obj   <- roc(as.numeric(y_test) - 1, pred_prob)
auc_value <- auc(roc_obj)
cat("AUC:", round(auc_value, 4), "\n")

best_threshold <- as.numeric(
  coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = "threshold")
)
cat("Optimal Threshold:", round(best_threshold, 4), "\n")

pred_class <- factor(
  ifelse(pred_prob >= best_threshold, "1", "0"),
  levels = c("0", "1")
)
y_test <- factor(y_test, levels = c("0", "1"))

cm <- confusionMatrix(pred_class, y_test, positive = "1")
f1 <- F1_Score(
  y_pred   = pred_class,
  y_true   = y_test,
  positive = "1"
)

mcc_value <- mcc(
  preds   = as.integer(pred_class) - 1,
  actuals = as.integer(y_test) - 1
)

tibble(
  Metric = c(
    "Accuracy",
    "AUC",
    "F1 Score",
    "Matthews Correlation Coefficient",
    "Sensitivity / Recall",
    "Specificity",
    "Precision",
    "Balanced Accuracy"
  ),
  Value = round(c(
    cm$overall["Accuracy"],
    as.numeric(auc_value),
    f1,
    mcc_value,
    cm$byClass["Sensitivity"],
    cm$byClass["Specificity"],
    cm$byClass["Pos Pred Value"],
    cm$byClass["Balanced Accuracy"]
  ), 4)
) |>
  kable(
    caption = "Elastic Net Binary Classification Performance",
    align   = c("l", "r")
  )

# Store binary F1 for comparison plot later
binary_f1 <- f1


# ROC Curve
plot(
  roc_obj,
  main = paste("ROC Curve — AUC:", round(auc_value, 3)),
  col  = "#224499",
  lwd  = 2,
  xlab = "False Positive Rate",
  ylab = "True Positive Rate"
)
abline(a = 1, b = 0, lty = 2, col = "gray60")


# Coefficients
coef_table <- data.frame(
  Predictor   = rownames(coef(elastic_net)),
  Coefficient = as.numeric(coef(elastic_net))
) |>
  filter(Predictor != "(Intercept)", abs(Coefficient) > 0.01) |>
  arrange(desc(abs(Coefficient)))

kable(
  coef_table,
  digits  = 4,
  caption = "Non-zero Elastic Net Coefficients"
)


# Confusion Matrix Plot
library(ggnewscale)
cm_plot <- data.frame(
  Actual    = c("Violent", "Property", "Violent", "Property"),
  Predicted = c("Violent", "Violent",  "Property", "Property"),
  Count     = c(19028, 56093, 9854, 64868),
  Type      = c("True Positive", "False Positive",
                "False Negative", "True Negative")
)
cm_plot$Percent <- round(100 * cm_plot$Count / sum(cm_plot$Count), 1)
cm_plot$Label   <- paste0(
  format(cm_plot$Count, big.mark = ","),
  "\n(", cm_plot$Percent, "%)\n",
  cm_plot$Type
)
cm_plot$correct <- cm_plot$Type %in% c("True Positive", "True Negative")
cm_plot$Actual    <- factor(cm_plot$Actual,    levels = c("Violent", "Property"))
cm_plot$Predicted <- factor(cm_plot$Predicted, levels = c("Property", "Violent"))

ggplot(cm_plot, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted)) +
  geom_tile(data = filter(cm_plot, correct),
            aes(fill = Count), color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#F5E6FF", high = "#DC267F", guide = "none") +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
  geom_tile(data = filter(cm_plot, !correct),
            aes(fill = Count), color = "white", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#E8F0FF", high = "#648FFF", guide = "none") +
  geom_text(aes(label = Label), fontface = "bold", size = 4.5) +
  labs(
    title    = "Confusion Matrix: Elastic Net",
    subtitle = paste0("threshold = ", round(best_threshold, 3)),
    x        = "Actual Class",
    y        = "Predicted Class"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    panel.grid  = element_blank(),
    plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 20),
    axis.title  = element_text(face = "bold")
  )




# Part 2: Multinomial (outcome Subtype)

valid_categories <- c(
  "Theft", "Vehicle Theft", "Vandalism",
  "Burglary", "Shoplifting",
  "Assault", "Robbery", "Sexual Offenses", "Other Violent"
)

multi_data <- clustered_incident_data |>
  select(outcome_subtype, all_of(predictors)) |>
  filter(outcome_subtype %in% valid_categories) |>
  na.omit() |>
  mutate(outcome_subtype = factor(outcome_subtype, levels = valid_categories))

multi_x <- model.matrix(outcome_subtype ~ ., data = multi_data)[, -1]
multi_y <- multi_data$outcome_subtype

set.seed(123)
multi_train_idx <- createDataPartition(multi_y, p = 0.80, list = FALSE)

multi_x_train <- multi_x[multi_train_idx, ]
multi_x_test  <- multi_x[-multi_train_idx, ]
multi_y_train <- multi_y[multi_train_idx]
multi_y_test  <- multi_y[-multi_train_idx]



# Inverse-frequency weights
class_counts  <- table(multi_y_train)
class_weights <- 1 / class_counts[as.character(multi_y_train)]
class_weights <- as.numeric(
  class_weights / sum(class_weights) * length(multi_y_train)
)

registerDoParallel(cores = parallel::detectCores() - 1)

multi_cv <- cv.glmnet(
  multi_x_train, multi_y_train,
  family       = "multinomial",
  alpha        = 0.5,
  nfolds       = 5,
  nlambda      = 50,
  type.measure = "deviance",
  weights      = class_weights,
  parallel     = TRUE
)
multi_lambda <- multi_cv$lambda.1se

stopImplicitCluster()

multi_enet <- glmnet(
  multi_x_train, multi_y_train,
  family  = "multinomial",
  alpha   = 0.5,
  lambda  = multi_lambda,
  weights = class_weights
)

multi_pred <- predict(
  multi_enet,
  newx = multi_x_test,
  s    = multi_lambda,
  type = "class"
) |> as.vector() |> factor(levels = levels(multi_y_test))

multi_cm <- confusionMatrix(multi_pred, multi_y_test)
print(multi_cm)

f1_per_class <- multi_cm$byClass[, "F1"]
cat("\nPer-Class F1:\n")
print(round(f1_per_class, 4))
cat("\nMacro F1:", round(mean(f1_per_class, na.rm = TRUE), 4), "\n")



# Coefficients
coef_list <- coef(multi_enet)

multi_coef_table <- lapply(names(coef_list), function(cls) {
  mat <- as.matrix(coef_list[[cls]])
  data.frame(
    Class       = cls,
    Predictor   = rownames(mat),
    Coefficient = as.numeric(mat)
  )
}) |>
  bind_rows() |>
  filter(Predictor != "(Intercept)", abs(Coefficient) > 0.01) |>
  arrange(Class, desc(abs(Coefficient)))

kable(
  multi_coef_table,
  digits  = 4,
  caption = "Non-zero Multinomial Elastic Net Coefficients by Class"
)


# Per-Subtype F1 Plot
f1_df <- data.frame(
  Subtype = gsub("Class: ", "", names(f1_per_class)),
  F1      = as.numeric(f1_per_class)
) |>
  filter(!is.na(F1)) |>
  mutate(
    CrimeType = case_when(
  Subtype %in% c("Assault", "Robbery", "Sexual Offenses",
                 "Other Violent") ~ "Violent",
  TRUE ~ "Property"
  ),
    Subtype = reorder(Subtype, F1)
  )

macro_f1 <- mean(f1_df$F1, na.rm = TRUE)

ggplot(f1_df, aes(x = F1, y = Subtype, fill = CrimeType)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = macro_f1,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "gray40",
    linewidth  = 0.7
  ) +
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = binary_f1,
    linetype   = "dashed",
    color      = "#4488FF",
    linewidth  = 0.7
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x     = macro_f1 + 0.005,
    y     = 1.5,
    label = paste0("Macro F1 = ", round(macro_f1, 3)),
    hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = "gray40"
  ) +
  annotate(
    "text",
    x     = binary_f1 + 0.005,
    y     = 3,
    label = paste0("Crime Type F1 = ", round(binary_f1, 3)),
    hjust = 1, size = 3.5, color = "#4488FF"
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
  values = c("Property" = "#785EF0", "Violent" = "#DC267F")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Per-Subtype F1 Score",
    subtitle = "Gray dashed = subtype macro F1; blue dashed = crime type F1",
    x        = "F1 Score",
    y        = NULL,
    fill     = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
  )
```

```{r subtype_metrics}
##overall multiclass MCC — coerce table to plain numeric matrix first
n_cls     <- length(levels(multi_y_test))
conf_num  <- matrix(as.numeric(multi_cm$table), nrow = n_cls, ncol = n_cls)
multi_mcc <- mcc(confusionM = conf_num)
cat("Multinomial Elastic Net MCC:", round(multi_mcc, 4), "\n")

##per-class MCC using one-vs-rest binarization
classes <- levels(multi_y_test)

per_class_mcc <- sapply(classes, function(cls) {
  binary_pred   <- as.integer(multi_pred == cls)
  binary_actual <- as.integer(multi_y_test == cls)
  mcc(preds = binary_pred, actuals = binary_actual)
})

##strip "Class: " prefix from confusionMatrix byClass rownames to match class labels
subtype_names <- gsub("Class: ", "", names(f1_per_class))

##combine F1 and per-class MCC into a single summary table
subtype_metrics <- data.frame(
  Subtype = subtype_names,
  F1      = round(as.numeric(f1_per_class), 4),
  MCC     = round(per_class_mcc[subtype_names], 4)
) |>
  arrange(desc(F1))

kable(
  subtype_metrics,
  caption   = "Per-Class F1 and MCC — Multinomial Elastic Net",
  align     = c("l", "r", "r"),
  col.names = c("Subtype", "F1 Score", "MCC")
)
```
